title: Remove timer for HealthCheck when container is paused
body:
This PR changes how containers are paused and unpaused.  Now, the timer for HealthCheck is removed when the container is paused. If the container is unpaused then a new timer for  HealthCheck is created.
 
### Reproducer:
1. `cid=$(podman run -dt --replace --name hc1 --health-interval 5s --healthcheck-command 'echo test' quay.io/libpod/alpine:latest)`
2. `podman pause hc1`
4. `systemctl --user status ${cid}-\*.{service,timer}`
> There should be no `systemctl` output. 

Fixes: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/24590
Fixes: https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RUN-2468

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md

```release-note
The HealthCheck timers are now removed when containers are paused and recreated when unpaused.
```
